claims.claim: Listing detail is the primary contact-host entry point and includes explicit no-booking/no-hold copy.
ContactHostButton starts or resumes a conversation and navigates to /messages/{conversationId} on success.
ContactHostButton can open a paywall dialog and post checkout offers to /api/payments/checkout.

startConversation enforces auth, rate limit, suspension, email verification, listing contactability, self-contact, host suspension, unit epoch, block, duplicate conversation, paywall, and contact-attempt behavior.
Message sending goes through sendMessage and sendConversationMessage with auth, rate, account, participant, listing, block, content flag, persistence, notification, and email paths.
GET and POST /api/messages are documented with auth, rate, access, polling, unread, mark-read, direct send, cache, and failure behavior.
claims.discrepancy: Source and focused Jest/API/security evidence support the documented behavior, including route-level missing-Origin CSRF rejection, but direct HTTP cache/header capture remains a P1 gap.
claims.recommendedFix: Run direct HTTP/API checks for status codes and cache headers before marking this fully pass.

claims.claim: The viewer-state API and public contact contract document contact CTA state, paywall summary, availability gates, private no-store behavior, and canBook/canHold false.
claims.discrepancy: Source contract fields are documented, but the full viewer-state route contract lacks focused runtime/status/cache tests.
claims.recommendedFix: Run or add focused viewer-state route tests for fields, statuses, cache headers, auth variants, and paywall conversion.

claims.claim: The data model and invariants cover conversation, message, notification, block, contact attempt, entitlement, consumption, listing, user, and physical unit state.
Auth/security docs distinguish protected and public/optional-auth surfaces and record source-observed permission gates.
Interaction census rows include trigger, code path, state/API effect, UI result, failure behavior, evidence, and current evidence-backed test status.
State model and diagrams cover viewer state, contact button, paywall, conversation, messages, read state, typing, block, deletion, checkout return, and realtime/polling state.

The test matrix separates release-blocking checks from confidence-building checks, records focused Jest/API/security/component, Chromium messaging, focused listing-detail Contact Host, Mobile Chrome no-deps, and setup-backed Mobile Chrome commands as passed, records the failed pre-fix and first-fix Mobile Chrome attempts as historical, and keeps broader E2E/runtime gaps explicit.
Runtime verification is explicitly marked PARTIALLY VERIFIED, with remaining unverified browser/runtime behavior not described as current verified behavior.
Message length mismatch is documented as a gap rather than resolved behavior.

The Phase 11 round-trip status reconciles historical Phase 1/2 and Phase 4 snapshots with current focused runtime/test evidence and preserves viewer-state full contract, checkout browser return, realtime/RLS, message-length, email, and full-matrix items as explicit P1/P2 gaps while adding schema/test-name detail for reconstruction.

coverageGaps.gap: Chromium dedupe passed earlier. Current verification passed Chromium messaging, focused listing-detail Contact Host runtime, Mobile Chrome no-deps, and setup-backed Mobile Chrome after stabilizing historical failures. Suspended/paywall/unavailable listing-detail states, checkout browser return, blocked UI browser flow, realtime/presence, email, and full browser matrix are not runtime verified.

Focused component/API/security/unit tests, focused MessagesPageClient tests, Chromium messaging E2E, focused listing-detail Contact Host E2E, Mobile Chrome messages no-deps, and setup-backed Mobile Chrome passed after the follow-up stabilization. The full browser matrix plus targeted checkout browser return/realtime tests have not been completed in this documentation pass.
A viewer-state private-feedback no-bleed test was discovered and added to the manifest, but no full focused route contract/status/cache test has been run for GET /api/listings/[id]/viewer-state.
Focused checkout and checkout-session API tests passed in CH-E044, but checkout browser return and runtime paid-unlock acceptance remain incomplete.
Realtime authorization/RLS and fallback behavior remain source-observed but not runtime verified.
Inbox, thread, and server/API limits differ and require product/test resolution.

CSRF helper behavior is documented from source, helper-level CSRF tests pass, and direct missing-Origin rejection tests pass for contact-host API paths; per-route malformed/mismatched Origin variants remain optional confidence coverage.
Email send calls are source-observed, but delivery behavior is not runtime verified.
